paper_title: Seismic and Site Data Analysis in the Scotia Sea

methods.description: This method involves the acquisition and processing of seismic data using Multi-channel Seismic (MCS) and Single-channel Seismic (SCS) profiles to study the southern Scotia Sea basins.

steps.description: A broad network of Multi-(MCS) and Single-channel Seismic (SCS) profiles exists over the southern Scotia Sea basins.
steps.searchable_summary: access seismic profiles data for southern Scotia Sea

steps.description: The seismic signal was recorded through a DELPH-2 system and processed with Radex-Pro.
steps.searchable_summary: process seismic signal using DELPH-2 and Radex-Pro

methods.description: This method involves correlating physical properties from wireline downhole and core-log measurements with seismic profiles to refine the interpretation of the sedimentary record in the Scotia Sea.

steps.description: Physical properties from wireline downhole and core-log measurements are correlated with the seismic profiles.
steps.searchable_summary: correlate physical properties with seismic profiles

steps.description: A time-depth relationship was established during the expedition allowing correlation between borehole stratigraphy and seismic reflection features.
steps.searchable_summary: establish time-depth relationship for borehole and seismic features

steps.description: Synthetic seismograms were created using the Petrel software package to select the best fit for the time-depth model.
steps.searchable_summary: create synthetic seismograms for model validation
